Club.title: Club
Club.description: Represents a sports club, acting as the root container for all sports-related data within the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Club entity.

name.description: The official name of the club.

address.description: The physical address of the club's main premises.

phone.description: The primary contact phone number for the club.

logoUrl.description: A URL pointing to the club's official logo image.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the club record was created.

Division.title: Division
Division.description: Represents a division or category within a club, typically organized by age groups (e.g., Sub 7, Sub 9).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Division entity.

ageMin.description: The minimum age for players in this division.

ageMax.description: The maximum age for players in this division.

clubId.description: Reference to the Club this division belongs to. (Relationship: Club 1:N Division)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the division record was created.

Team.title: Team
Team.description: Represents a specific team within a division (e.g., 'Sub11 A').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Team entity.

name.description: The name of the team (e.g., 'Sub11 A', 'Juniors B').

divisionId.description: Reference to the Division this team belongs to. (Relationship: Division 1:N Team)

coachName.description: The name of the primary coach for this team.

season.description: The sports season to which this team record applies (e.g., '2023-2024').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the team record was created.

definition.description: Root collection for all sports clubs. Each document represents a single club. Access typically requires global admin roles or specific club ownership/membership permissions (which would be added to the document or a related roles collection).

params.name: clubId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific Club.

definition.description: Subcollection for divisions belonging to a specific club. Includes denormalized 'clubId' for authorization independence, allowing rules to directly check parent club context.

params.name: clubId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Club.

params.name: divisionId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific Division within the Club.

definition.description: Subcollection for teams belonging to a specific division and club. Includes denormalized 'clubId' (which should be added to the Team entity schema) and 'divisionId' for robust authorization independence, enabling direct context checks within security rules.

params.name: clubId
params.description: Unique identifier of the grandparent Club.

params.name: divisionId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Division.

params.name: teamId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific Team within the Division.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure adheres strictly to the core design principles, prioritizing Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. The primary design strategy is based on **hierarchical path-based ownership** coupled with **strategic denormalization** of authorization context.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This is achieved by embedding parent identifiers directly within child documents. For `Division` documents, the `clubId` is present in the document itself (as specified in the schema), which matches the `{clubId}` wildcard in its path `/clubs/{clubId}/divisions/{divisionId}`. This allows security rules for divisions to evaluate authorization based on `resource.data.clubId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent club document.
Crucially, for `Team` documents, while the user's initial schema included `divisionId`, for true Authorization Independence, the `clubId` must also be present directly within the `Team` document. This allows rules to check `resource.data.clubId` directly for team-level access, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to the parent `Division` document to retrieve its `clubId`. This ensures atomic operations (transactions, batches) involving teams and their related club authorizations are robust and debuggable. The path `clubs/{clubId}/divisions/{divisionId}/teams/{teamId}` further reinforces this, making the parent `clubId` and `divisionId` available as path variables. The `description` for the `Team` path explicitly mentions this denormalization.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structural segregation is key to enabling secure `list` operations (Query Against Paths). By organizing data into `clubs/{clubId}/divisions` and `clubs/{clubId}/divisions/{divisionId}/teams`, we ensure that all documents within a given subcollection share a homogeneous security posture. For instance, to retrieve all divisions for a specific club, an authenticated user only needs to query `/clubs/{clubId}/divisions`. The security rules can then easily verify if `request.auth.uid` has access to `clubId` (e.g., if `request.auth.uid` is an administrator or member of `clubId` as determined by a `members` map or `ownerId` on the club document itself). This prevents over-fetching data and applying client-side filtering, making queries efficient and secure. The denormalized `clubId` in `Division` and `Team` documents facilitates these checks directly within the document, rather than relying on parent references that would prevent `list` queries from being efficiently secured.
